items.title: New Victorian laws could see businesses shut down if they're caught selling illicit tobacco
items.summary: The Victorian government will introduce new laws giving the tobacco licensing regulator the power to shut down businesses caught selling illegal tobacco.

items.title: Plea from Tasmania's police commissioner for limit on gun ownership knocked back by government
items.summary: In the push for gun law reform in the wake of the Bondi attack, Tasmania's police commissioner urged the state government to limit the number of firearms a person can own, citing the murder of an officer the year before in her plea — but to no avail, documents show.

items.title: Perth family locked in battle with NDIS over care for disabled son
items.summary: The parents of a disabled teenager have lifted the lid on the toll lobbying the NDIS for adequate support has taken on their lives, saying they feel exhausted and let down.

items.title: Barnaby Joyce regrets not doing more to shore up fuel stockpile, amid inflation fears
items.summary: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce says he regrets not doing more when he was in government to secure fuel reserves, as Australia grapples with supply shortages triggered by war in the Middle East.

items.title: If you feel like the world is spinning too quickly, look at who's setting the pace
items.summary: Around the world, things are moving at a dizzying speed, and the pace is being set from Washington. Some people claim this is a deliberate strategy to overwhelm us, and that it comes with consequences.

items.title: Cracks appear in Runit Dome amid sea level rise in Marshall Islands
items.summary: Cracks have appeared in a concrete dome holding 120,000 tonnes of contaminated soil and debris from Cold War-era nuclear bomb tests.

items.title: New discovery of rare Lord Howe Island stick insect colonies
items.summary: Once so common they were used as bait, Lord Howe Island stick insects are making a comeback from the brink of extinction.

items.title: Eight players on Tony Popovic's radar to make the Socceroos 26-man 2026 World Cup squad
items.summary: Tony Popovic wants his players to show "toughness" as all Socceroos sides have done in the past, but he wants them to shed the underdog tag. These players could help Australia do that at the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Inside Lainey Wilson's non‑stop rise through tours, Netflix doco, Stagecoach and new single
items.summary: Arena tours, feature films and a new single — here's how one of America's biggest stars keeps climbing.

items.title: First Australian woman finishes Oceans Seven swimming challenge despite shark attack
items.summary: Joanne Norman cinched the international accolade by swimming across seven of the world's most dangerous channels.

items.title: UAE minister Reem Al Hashimy describes Iran's attacks as 'almost unhinged', tells ABC they surprised her
items.summary: Iran's actions in attacking neighbouring countries were unprecedented, "almost unhinged" and irrational, according to a senior United Arab Emirates minister.

items.title: Message in a bottle discovery on Tasmanian beach leads to 25-year intercontinental friendship
items.summary: One night in 1997, fuelled by boredom, trainee ship bartender Erika Boyero filled some empty bottles with handwritten notes and threw them overboard in Norway. Last week, she met with the Tasmanian woman who found one of her messages.

items.title: Fly larvae turns Riverland food and farm waste into animal feed
items.summary: A regional South Australian couple's fly larvae farm is using discarded produce to make a valuable food source.

items.title: Loved ones of Australian woman question backpacker Tobias Pick's manslaughter sentence
items.summary: In 2020, a German backpacker strangled a Tasmanian sex worker, then fled with her mobile phone and cash. His version of events was accepted by an Australian court, which jailed him for manslaughter. Now, German authorities are re-examining the case.

items.title: From petrol prices to mining, here's how the Iran war is affecting WA
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is beginning to have a tangible impact on the lives of Western Australians. Here's how.

items.title: Farmers hit with rising costs and lost production as battle against rabbit crisis worsens
items.summary: Farmers say they are killing thousands of wild rabbits on their properties, but the problem is getting worse and they are seeking help.

items.title: Election campaigning in an ankle bracelet, Nick McBride wants a third term in MacKillop
items.summary: ABC News joined Nick McBride on the campaign trail in Lucindale, a small sheep and cattle farming town 345 kilometres south-east of Adelaide, where locals gathered at the footy oval for the annual show.

items.title: Scammers target Australians with 'free' tai chi classes ad to trick them into downloading malware
items.summary: Scammers are targeting Australians with advertisements for 'free' tai chi classes to trick them into downloading malware capable of stealing money off their devices.

items.title: Three members of Iranian women's football team return home after seeking asylum
items.summary: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke confirms that a trio of members of the Iranian women's football team, who were given humanitarian visas, chose to return home.

items.title: Are we outsourcing our souls to artificial intelligence?
items.summary: More people are turning to AI companions during personal crises, treating it as a confidante and even a way to speak to the dead. And while it helps some, it can also have devastating consequences, writes Compass presenter Benjamin Law.

items.title: How the Iranian-Australian community united to help Iran's women footballers
items.summary: The remaining members of Iran's women's football team might have left Australia, but the journey is far from over, as members of the Iranian-Australian community share what really went into the fight to protect their countrywomen.

items.title: Arnhem Land called Yothu Yindi co-founder Witiyana Marika home as band was going global
items.summary: Rockstar. Leader. Peacemaker. Witiyana Marika was riding the wave of international fame in the band Yothu Yindi when his dying father called him back to Arnhem Land.

items.title: Is Australia about to say goodbye to its experiment with low unemployment?
items.summary: If war in the Middle East sends global inflation surging, will Australia have to say goodbye to its experiment with low unemployment?

items.title: The new health-boosting wheat ready to change the Western diet
items.summary: Australian scientists have developed a wheat variety that dramatically boosts fibre in white bread and other flour-based foods.

items.title: AI is helping choose targets in Iran war — now it's a target too
items.summary: Iran struck three data centres in the Middle East, and has signalled that technology infrastructure will continue to be in the firing line.

items.title: Statement from Iran's new leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, fuels rumours about his whereabouts
items.summary: Late last week, Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, broke his silence after days of intensifying rumours about his health and whereabouts, but it did little to put them to rest.

items.title: Meet Margaret Thanos, director of A Mirror and The River and a rising star of Australian theatre
items.summary: With two main-stage productions already under her belt in 2026, Margaret Thanos has cemented her place as a rising star of Australian theatre.

items.title: Back Roads: In Eldorado, Myf Warhurst meets the king of compost toilets
items.summary: When Myf Warhurst arrived in the sleepy town of Eldorado to film the latest episode of Back Roads, she didn't anticipate she would be touching compost made from human waste. But Hamish Skermer has that effect.

items.title: Hard Quiz: Time to show us what you've got. Let's play, HARD!
items.summary: One of these days, you might get lucky and ace this quiz. It's unlikely to be today. Let's play, HARD!

items.title: Political risks of war against Iran go beyond the petrol pump
items.summary: If the instability in the Middle East keeps energy markets volatile, the economic and political consequences will extend far beyond the petrol pump.

items.title: The Bikers Against Child Abuse helping kids reclaim a sense of safety
items.summary: BACA members accompany kids to court, stand guard outside their homes, and provide a sense of safety with burly presences and deeply rumbling motorbikes.

items.title: Trump threatens to hit Iran's Kharg Island oil network if shipping lanes remain blocked
items.summary: US President Donald Trump threatens to strike the oil infrastructure of Iran's Kharg Island hub unless Tehran stopped attacking vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Queensland Reds defeat NSW Waratahs 26-17 in Super Rugby Pacific round 5 clash at Lang Park
items.summary: The Queensland Reds have continued their winning run in Super Rugby, defeating arch-rival NSW Waratahs in a contest that lacked quality for an hour, then produced exhilarating play for the final 20 minutes.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas learn semifinal opponent as Chinese Taipei fans grapple with team name
items.summary: The Matildas will face China PR in the semifinals of the Women's Asian Cup after the Steel Roses required extra time to see off a disciplined and spirited Chinese Taipei at Perth Rectangular Stadium.

items.title: Indonesian human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus attacked with acid on Jakarta street
items.summary: A prominent human rights activist has been attacked with acid in Jakarta, leaving him with serious burns.

items.title: One Nation's Cory Bernardi talks 'rookie error', Liberal MP dissents on candidate controversy ahead of South Australian election
items.summary: A week out from election day in SA, One Nation's Cory Bernardi admits to a "rookie error", as a federal Liberal MP expresses reservations about the decision to part ways with a controversial candidate.

items.title: Man arrested after body found inside home in Wellington, NSW
items.summary: A man has been arrested after an older man was found dead and a woman critically injured inside a home at Wellington, New South Wales on Saturday.

items.title: What is Iran's Kharg Island used for and why has the US attacked it?
items.summary: Donald Trump says the US has "obliterated" Iran's military assets on the island, but stopped short of attacking other infrastructure.

items.title: At 102 years old, Dean Simes might be Australia's oldest tech-help guy
items.summary: Dean Simes bought his first personal computer this century. Now, the centenarian wants to help other seniors understand the technology "you can't avoid".

items.title: 'Blowing my mind': Panthers thump Sharks in brutal Bathurst contest
items.summary: The clash between Penrith and Cronulla blows the mind of former coach Matthew Elliott as the Panthers dominate a physical affair in Bathurst.

items.title: New fuel standards will see higher levels of sulphur in Australia's petrol. What does that mean for your car?
items.summary: The federal government's temporary easing of fuel standards will see 200 million litres of "dirty" fuel injected into the domestic market. Should drivers be worried?

items.title: How Middle East airport closures are affecting foreign travellers
items.summary: John McGuigan was due to go back to work weeks ago in Manchester. He's still stuck in Australia using up all his work leave entitlements due to the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Trump posts video of strikes on Kharg Island
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Majority of the Gulf's migrants are South Asian — their families fear for their safety and free speech
items.summary: For many South Asian migrants across the Gulf, keeping quiet about Iran's attacks ensures they're able to pay the bills and feed their families. But is leaving even an option?

items.title: Buffel grass fight reaches Canberra as cattle industry pushes back
items.summary: Traditional owners and scientists want buffel grass recognised as a Weed of National Significance, but the cattle industry opposes the move because it relies on the species for fodder.

items.title: Biscuit, tiramisu and pistachio stuffing: dates just got even better - ABC News
items.summary: Forget chocolate bars, these stuffed dates are an easy treat and also make a beautiful gift.

items.title: Injuries mar Swans' thumping win over premiers
items.summary: Sydney cruise to a second big home win and condemn reigning AFL premiers Brisbane to a second loss, but pay a price with injuries.while Adelaide records an impressive victory over Collingwood at the MCG.

items.title: Alleged Darwin drug dealers charged, interstate drug syndicate targeted in arrests
items.summary: A months-long interstate police operation targeting alleged methamphetamine supply in Darwin has ended with a bust netting more than 156 kilograms of illicit drugs.

items.title: Biofuel industry awaits Commonwealth support as fuel tensions increase in Middle East
items.summary: Australia once produced 80 per cent of its own fuel. Now it imports the same amount, and an unlikely answer may be sitting in your local takeaway shop.

items.title: Rotating home owners boast of 360-degree views and energy benefits
items.summary: They may be costly to build, but rotating homes are loved for the way they can move with the sun, capture breezes from any direction and make the most of the scenery around them.

items.title: WA politicians chided for 'appalling' behaviour in Question Time. Don't we deserve better?
items.summary: Question Time in WA is often loud, occasionally funny, and rarely productive. If this is how the people elected to look out for the best interests of West Australians behave at work, don't we deserve better?

items.title: Cultural burning practices return to Yuwibara Country for first time since colonisation
items.summary: A practice that has helped care for land for more than 60,000 years returns to Yuwibara Country in north Queensland.

items.title: Why does denim attract hordes of biting march flies?
items.summary: Denim-wearing Louise Stokes was swarmed by the biting insects during a bushwalk near Pemberton, and it turns out clothing choice can play a significant role in deterring them.

items.title: Outback band launches debut EP inspired by language and country
items.summary: A group of young musicians from Queensland's Gulf country are turning stories and language into song, and are now set mentor other aspiring performers.

items.title: Adrian Segečić switches from Australia to Croatia ahead of FIFA World Cup
items.summary: One of Australia's brightest young talents appears to have been lost without even playing a game for the Socceroos, with Portsmouth dynamo Adrian Segečić switching associations to Croatia.

items.title: Health authorities investigating source of mysterious eye infections in Darwin football players
items.summary: Aussie Rules players in Darwin with symptoms of conjunctivitis are being urged to contact health authorities amid an outbreak of unknown origin affecting dozens of players.

items.title: NT parliament increases minimum sentence for domestic violence murder, speaker berates backbencher
items.summary: Northern Territory parliament has passed legislation increasing the mandatory minimum prison sentence for domestic violence murder from 20 to 25 years. During sittings this week, the speaker also berated a government backbencher over his handling of bullying claims.

items.title: Police allege newspaper boss Antony Catalano attacked woman with clothes iron
items.summary: The co-owner of national newspaper company ACM has been stood down from his position after appearing in court, with police alleging he assaulted a woman and swung a clothes iron at her head.

items.title: Wayne Bennett calls pitch invasion after Alex Johnston's NRL try-scoring record 'pretty disruptive'
items.summary: A grumpy Wayne Bennett has told reporters he has already answered enough questions about Alex Johnston's try-scoring record, and described fans rushing the field as "pretty disruptive".

items.title: Couran Cove resident dismayed as court upholds $22m body corp debt
items.summary: A resident of the Couran Cove Island Resort in the northern Gold Coast says she is shocked by a court decision upholding a $22 million body corporate debt.

items.title: Confusion and anger as Bluesfest ticketholders question timing of cancellation
items.summary: Festival revellers, who have spent thousands on the event, are angry and have been left wondering when organisers knew it was not viable.
